text:
To determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit, we first need to calculate the total number of eggs she will need to make 15 Spanish flans. Since each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs, we can find the total number of eggs needed by multiplying the number of flans by the number of eggs per flan:

\[ 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs/flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Next, we need to determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit to get 45 eggs. Since her neighbor gives her a basket of 9 eggs each time she babysits, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs per babysitting session:

\[ \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs/babysitting}} = 5 \text{ babysitting sessions} \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times
